---
title: "Online Supplement for CRPTO"
author: "Anonymous"
date: today
lang: en
bibliography: ../book/references.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    number-sections: true
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
execute:
  freeze: auto
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Scope

This supplement is the IJDS online-supplement scaffold. It should absorb the
material that is too long for the 25-page body but still necessary for reviewer
trust: proofs, robustness tables A3--A18, reproducibility, MRM, fairness and
artifact lineage.
:::

# Appendix A: Theoretical Details

- Markov bound proof for fixed allocation.
- Conditional Hoeffding/Bernstein tightening and dependence caveat.
- Notation table: `Y_i`, `p_hat_i`, `u_i(alpha)`, `x_i`, `w_i`, `Gamma_CP`,
  `gamma`, `V`.

# Appendix B: P1 Evidence

Include or reference:

- A3 nested holdout;
- A4 segment-period sensitivity;
- A5 decision-aware conformal selector;
- A6 synthetic shift;
- A7 funded-set loan export;
- A8 funded-set composition;
- A9 strict temporal holdout;
- A10 conformal finalist exact bound evaluation;
- A11 enhanced synthetic shift.

# Appendix C: Journal Robustness Package

Include or reference:

- A12 tail-risk OCE/CVaR diagnostics;
- A13 satisficing margins;
- A14 dependency cluster diagnostics;
- A15 leave-one-period stress;
- A16 bootstrap funded-set metrics;
- A17 budget/LGD/cap sensitivity;
- A18 robust region by policy family.

# Appendix D: Fair Lending, MRM And Governance

Summarize the proxy/intersectional fairness audit, MRM card, challenger policy
and retraining triggers. State clearly that the open Lending Club data does not
provide direct protected attributes for legal fairness certification.

# Appendix E: Reproducibility

List the minimal reviewer commands:

```bash
just setup-base
just smoke
just tables
just figures
just journal-package
just book
```

Also include:

- DVC remote and artifact policy after anonymity handling;
- MLflow/DagsHub run lineage after anonymity handling;
- release tag and manifest hash policy;
- known slow stages and stages that must not be rerun without drift validation.
